root_text.an10.96:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ānando rājagahe viharati tapodārāme.
root_text.an10.96:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā ānando rattiyā paccūsasamayaṁ paccuṭṭhāya yena tapodā tenupasaṅkami gattāni parisiñcituṁ.
root_text.an10.96:1.3: Tapodāya gattāni parisiñcitvā paccuttaritvā ekacīvaro aṭṭhāsi gattāni pubbāpayamāno.
root_text.an10.96:1.4: Kokanudopi kho paribbājako rattiyā paccūsasamayaṁ paccuṭṭhāya yena tapodā tenupasaṅkami gattāni parisiñcituṁ.
root_text.an10.96:2.1: Addasā kho kokanudo paribbājako āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ dūratova āgacchantaṁ.
root_text.an10.96:4.1: “Puccheyyāma mayaṁ āyasmantaṁ kiñcideva desaṁ, sace āyasmā okāsaṁ karoti pañhassa veyyākaraṇāyā”ti.
root_text.an10.96:5.1: “Kiṁ nu kho, bho, ‘sassato loko, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan’ti—evaṁdiṭṭhi bhavan”ti?
root_text.an10.96:6.1: “Kiṁ pana, bho, ‘asassato loko, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan’ti—evaṁdiṭṭhi bhavan”ti?
root_text.an10.96:7.7: hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā …
root_text.an10.96:7.8: neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññanti—evaṁdiṭṭhi bhavan”ti?
root_text.an10.96:7.10: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan’”ti.
root_text.an10.96:8.1: “Tena hi bhavaṁ na jānāti, na passatī”ti?
root_text.an10.96:8.2: “Na kho ahaṁ, āvuso, na jānāmi na passāmi.
root_text.an10.96:9.1: “‘Kiṁ nu kho, bho, sassato loko, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññanti—
root_text.an10.96:9.4: sassato loko, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan’ti vadesi.
root_text.an10.96:10.1: ‘Kiṁ pana, bho, asassato loko, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññanti—
root_text.an10.96:10.4: asassato loko, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan’ti vadesi.
root_text.an10.96:11.1: Kiṁ nu kho, bho, antavā loko …pe…
root_text.an10.96:11.7: hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā …
root_text.an10.96:11.8: neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññanti—
root_text.an10.96:11.11: neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan’ti vadesi.
root_text.an10.96:12.1: ‘Tena hi bhavaṁ na jānāti na passatī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.an10.96:12.2: ‘na kho ahaṁ, āvuso, na jānāmi na passāmi.
root_text.an10.96:12.4: Yathā kathaṁ panāvuso, imassa bhāsitassa attho daṭṭhabbo”ti?
root_text.an10.96:13.1: “‘Sassato loko, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan’ti kho, āvuso, diṭṭhigatametaṁ.
root_text.an10.96:13.2: ‘Asassato loko, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan’ti kho, āvuso, diṭṭhigatametaṁ.
root_text.an10.96:13.9: hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā …
root_text.an10.96:13.10: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan’ti kho, āvuso, diṭṭhigatametaṁ.
root_text.an10.96:14.1: Yāvatā, āvuso, diṭṭhi yāvatā diṭṭhiṭṭhānaṁ diṭṭhiadhiṭṭhānaṁ diṭṭhipariyuṭṭhānaṁ diṭṭhisamuṭṭhānaṁ diṭṭhisamugghāto, tamahaṁ jānāmi tamahaṁ passāmi.
root_text.an10.96:14.2: Tamahaṁ jānanto tamahaṁ passanto kyāhaṁ vakkhāmi:
root_text.an10.96:15.1: “Ko nāmo āyasmā, kathañca panāyasmantaṁ sabrahmacārī jānantī”ti?
root_text.an10.96:15.4: “Mahācariyena vata kira, bho, saddhiṁ mantayamānā na jānimha:

translation_text.an10.96:1.1: At one time Venerable Ānanda was staying near Rājagaha in the Hot Springs Monastery.
translation_text.an10.96:1.2: Then Ānanda rose at the crack of dawn and went to the hot springs to bathe.
translation_text.an10.96:1.3: When he had bathed and emerged from the water he stood in one robe drying his limbs.
translation_text.an10.96:1.4: The wanderer Kokanada also rose at the crack of dawn and went to the hot springs to bathe.
translation_text.an10.96:2.1: He saw Ānanda coming off in the distance
translation_text.an10.96:3.2: “Of the ascetics who follow the Sakyan.”
translation_text.an10.96:4.1: “I’d like to ask the venerable about a certain point, if you’d take the time to answer.”
translation_text.an10.96:4.2: “Ask, reverend. When I’ve heard it I’ll know.”
translation_text.an10.96:5.1: “Is this your view: ‘The cosmos is eternal. This is the only truth, anything else is futile’?”
translation_text.an10.96:6.1: “Then is this your view: ‘The cosmos is not eternal. This is the only truth, anything else is futile’?”
translation_text.an10.96:7.1: “Then is this your view: ‘The cosmos is finite …’ …
translation_text.an10.96:7.3: ‘The soul and the body are one and the same …’ …
translation_text.an10.96:7.4: ‘The soul is one thing, the body another …’ …
translation_text.an10.96:7.5: ‘A realized one still exists after death …’ …
translation_text.an10.96:7.6: ‘A realized one no longer exists after death …’ …
translation_text.an10.96:7.7: ‘A realized one both still exists and no longer exists after death …’ …
translation_text.an10.96:7.8: ‘A Realized One neither exists nor doesn’t exist after death. This is the only truth, anything else is futile’?”
translation_text.an10.96:8.1: “Then, sir, do you neither know nor see?”
translation_text.an10.96:9.1: “When asked about all these points, you say that’s not your view.
translation_text.an10.96:12.1: Yet when asked whether you neither know nor see,
translation_text.an10.96:12.4: How then should we see the meaning of this statement?”
translation_text.an10.96:13.1: “‘The cosmos is eternal. This is the only truth, anything else is futile:’ that’s a misconception.
translation_text.an10.96:13.2: ‘The cosmos is not eternal. This is the only truth, anything else is futile:’ that’s a misconception.
translation_text.an10.96:13.5: ‘The soul and the body are one and the same …’ …
translation_text.an10.96:13.6: ‘The soul is one thing, the body another …’ …
translation_text.an10.96:13.7: ‘A realized one still exists after death …’ …
translation_text.an10.96:13.8: ‘A realized one no longer exists after death …’ …
translation_text.an10.96:13.9: ‘A realized one both still exists and no longer exists after death …’ …
translation_text.an10.96:13.10: ‘A Realized One neither exists nor doesn’t exist after death. This is the only truth, anything else is futile:’ that’s a misconception.
translation_text.an10.96:14.1: I know and see the scope of convictions, the scope of grounds for views, fixation on views, obsession with views, the origin of views, and the uprooting of views.
translation_text.an10.96:14.2: Knowing and seeing thus, why should I say:
translation_text.an10.96:15.1: “What is the venerable’s name? And how are you known among your spiritual companions?”
translation_text.an10.96:15.3: And that’s how I’m known among my spiritual companions.”
translation_text.an10.96:15.4: “Goodness! I had no idea I was consulting such a great tutor as
translation_text.an10.96:15.7: you were, I wouldn’t have said so much.
translation_text.an10.96:15.8: May Venerable Ānanda please forgive me.”

variant_text.an10.96:2.3: kvettha → ko’ttha (bj); ko tettha (si); kvattha (pts1ed, mr)
variant_text.an10.96:14.1: diṭṭhi → diṭṭhigatā (sabbattha) | yāvatā diṭṭhiṭṭhānaṁ diṭṭhiadhiṭṭhānaṁ diṭṭhipariyuṭṭhānaṁ diṭṭhisamuṭṭhānaṁ diṭṭhisamugghāto → yāvatā diṭṭhiṭṭhānā adhiṭṭhānaṁ pariyuṭṭhānaṁ samugghāto (sya-all); yāvatā diṭṭhiṭṭhāna adhiṭṭhāna pariyuṭṭhāna samuṭṭhāna samugghāto (pts1ed)
